message.content:
Fix the Stage-0 lineage seam per Sol's diff-gate NO-GO. The seam is UNCOMMITTED in /home/polaris/wt/faithoff (6 modified files: gate0_lineage.py, run_gate_b.py, run_validity_gate.py, run_honest_sweep_r3.py, score_report_race.py, corpus_snapshot.py + 2 test files under tests/dr_benchmark/). Work DIRECTLY in /home/polaris/wt/faithoff on those existing uncommitted changes (do NOT create a new worktree, do NOT revert them — build ON them). Do NOT commit. Do NOT run any report generation or scoring (no spend). Report the updated diff + test results when done.

Read first: Sol's full verdict `/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/docs/race_fact_initiative/stage0_diffgate_sol_verdict.md` (the authoritative fix list), the design `docs/race_fact_initiative/stage0_lineage_seam_spec_v2.md`, and the guardrail `docs/race_fact_initiative/GHOST_BAN.md` (stay pure lineage plumbing — no admission/entailment/binding/post-gen/content lever; run the GHOST_BAN grep on your final diff).

Apply EXACTLY these 4 fixes (all grounded in Sol's verdict; keep the DEFAULT path byte-identical to HEAD throughout):

FIX 1 — No-gold fail-open (Sol item 2): the direct-sweep legacy branch (`run_honest_sweep_r3.py:21971-21982`) only rejects `_slug in SLUG_TO_IDX`, so an explicit no-gold benchmark slug like `drb_90_adas_liability` (a benchmark with no legacy mapping) passes through and runs unchanged. Make legacy registration/checking reject EVERY benchmark slug absent from `SLUG_TO_LEGACY_TASK` (including explicit no-gold slugs) via a shared resolver/registration helper in gate0_lineage.py, and call that SAME check from BOTH Gate-B and the direct sweep. Preserve Gate-B's existing correct `is_benchmark_slug` rejection (`run_gate_b.py:5732-5741`).

FIX 2 — Raw-byte equality (Sol item 3): the "raw registered question == legacy canonical" checks (`run_gate_b.py:5716-5717`, `run_honest_sweep_r3.py:21963-21964`) compare only `sha256_text`, which normalizes whitespace (`gate0_lineage.py:144-151`). Assert RAW string equality AND normalized-SHA equality where v2 requires both (the registered legacy question, and the packed/answered/canonical evidence). REPLACE the whitespace-drift-acceptance test (`test_stage0_lineage_seam.py:197-206` passes "LEGACY   Q"/"LEGACY Q"/"LEGACY\nQ" as equal) with explicit raw-equality PLUS sha-equality tests.

FIX 3 — Coverage gate hits the WRONG predicate (Sol item 4 — MOST IMPORTANT): the change to `_required_entity_ledger_failed_under_strict` (`run_honest_sweep_r3.py:1912-1939`) only controls whether an EXCEPTION building the RequiredEntityLedger becomes the F27 abort — NOT the native required-element coverage decision. The actual below-threshold coverage HOLD is created at `release_policy.py:235-254`, fed by `native_gate_b_inputs.py:283-301,959-960,1228-1231`. So a legacy run can STILL abort post-spend on real coverage shortfall. Fix: preserve the native coverage fraction/gaps/audit UNCHANGED; make ONLY the idx-56 required-element COVERAGE-SHORTFALL blocker report-only for `legacy_race_task` at an outer lineage-aware disposition seam (the release_policy HOLD seam). Do NOT relax claim faithfulness, S0/fabrication/zero-grounding decisions, or edit content. KEEP a RequiredEntityLedger IMPLEMENTATION failure fail-loud/durably disclosed (do not silently convert to WARN-only success — REVERT that part of the prior change if needed). Add a behavioral test: a below-threshold native fixed-denominator result → default BLOCKS as before; legacy retains the exact coverage telemetry/gap but does NOT abort for that reason alone.

FIX 4 — Resume lineage mismatch direction (Sol item 6): `run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9554-9559` passes `expected_lineage=q.get("question_lineage")` = None for default queries, so `corpus_snapshot.py:198-207` does no check → a stored LEGACY snapshot is wrongly accepted when a later run selects DEFAULT. Fix: at the production resume seam, treat an ABSENT query marker as the effective `drb_ii_idx` lineage for the comparison (while still omitting the default lineage from serialized artifacts). Add caller-level tests for BOTH mismatch directions (default-stored→legacy-run AND legacy-stored→default-run).

ALSO strengthen tests per Sol's "test-harness incompleteness": add a behavioral test asserting the SAME task-72 raw value/SHA carries through protocol.research_question, retrieval seed, compile_frame, contract plan, generator arg, H1, snapshot, and scorer pack (single-brain flow); add a scorer-guard test; make the default golden-identity test compare selected `q` + protocol bytes to the HEAD-equivalent, not just manifest shape.

Constraints: keep the forced `PG_BENCHMARK_OFFICIAL_QUESTION="1"` + required-flags tuple byte-identical; default path stays byte-identical to HEAD; faithfulness engine (provenance_generator.py, strict_verify.py) untouched/not imported; no magic counts / adjective-version flag names; no ghost. Re-locate any line number that has shifted (grep) — never guess.

When done: run `/home/polaris/conda_cu128/bin/python -m pytest tests/dr_benchmark/test_stage0_lineage_seam.py tests/dr_benchmark/test_stage0_lineage_sweep_integration.py -q` (must be green, including the new tests), run the GHOST_BAN grep on the full diff, confirm `git diff -- '**/provenance_generator.py' '**/strict_verify.py'` is EMPTY, and confirm no NEW regressions vs HEAD in `tests/dr_benchmark/` (stash-compare if useful). REPORT: which files/functions changed for each of the 4 fixes, new/changed tests + pass count, GHOST_BAN grep result, and any fix you could not apply cleanly (flag it, don't force it). Leave everything uncommitted for the re-gate.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Stage-0 lineage seam — Sol diff-gate verdict
2	
3	## Verdict
4	
5	**NO-GO to commit and NO-GO to run the re-baseline.**
6	
7	The default `drb_ii_idx` path is clean and the mapped task-72 legacy happy path is mostly
8	single-brained, but the implementation does not fully match the reconciled v2 design:
9	
10	1. the direct-sweep legacy branch fails open for an explicitly registered no-gold benchmark slug
11	   with no legacy mapping;
12	2. the purported legacy “coverage report-only” change alters the wrong predicate (a
13	   RequiredEntityLedger implementation-error abort), not the native fixed-denominator
14	   required-element coverage decision;
15	3. raw-byte equality is not enforced/tested as required; the production comparisons use only a
16	   SHA over whitespace-normalized text, and a new test explicitly accepts raw drift;
17	4. the sweep caller does not enforce the snapshot lineage mismatch in the legacy-to-default
18	   direction (the question-SHA guard happens to catch task-72 because the two questions differ,
19	   but the required lineage invariant is incomplete);
20	5. the new tests do not behaviorally exercise all advertised seams or the scorer.
21	
22	No generation or scoring spend should start until these are fixed and re-diff-gated.
23	
24	## Per-item results
25	
26	### 1. DEFAULT-PATH BYTE IDENTITY — PASS
27	
28	Grounded code review:
29	
30	- `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:215-244` keeps the positional
31	  `(slug, tasks_path)` call valid and selects the unchanged DRB-II loader on the default branch.
32	- `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:5688` still force-assigns
33	  `PG_BENCHMARK_OFFICIAL_QUESTION="1"`.
34	- `_BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_REQUIRED_FLAGS` still contains that flag exactly once at
35	  `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:2064-2070`; the selector is not added to the tuple.
36	- The selector allowlist at `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:4824-4842` is inert when unset and
37	  accepts explicit `drb_ii_idx`.
38	- The default Gate-B override remains the old canonical-idx branch at
39	  `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:5742-5750`; it adds no lineage key.
40	- `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_validity_gate.py:128-144` loads the normal output contract for unset
41	  or `drb_ii_idx`.
42	- `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:15847-15860` passes `lineage=None` on the default path, and
43	  `src/polaris_graph/generator/corpus_snapshot.py:134-137` therefore adds no snapshot key.
44	- `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:351-374` preserves the default manifest shape and key
45	  order, including `canonical_idx`, with no `lineage` key.
46	- `scripts/score_report_race.py:64-69` adds only a stdlib gate0 import/env read on default; the
47	  legacy scoring block is not entered.
48	
49	Read-only deterministic HEAD-vs-worktree harness results:
50	
51	- default canonical resolver output: exact;
52	- default lineage-manifest dict and serialized JSON: exact;
53	- default snapshot bytes: exact, with no `lineage` key;
54	- output-contract result: exact for unset and explicit `drb_ii_idx`;
55	- required-flags tuple: exact;
56	- default coverage-helper truth table: exact for unset and explicit `drb_ii_idx`;
57	- default resolver opened only the DRB-II task file and did not open `query.jsonl`.
58	
59	No default-path artifact or behavior divergence was found for the stated surfaces.
60	
61	### 2. SINGLE-BRAIN COMPLETENESS FOR LEGACY — FAIL
62	
63	What is correct for mapped task 72:
64	
65	- Resolver: `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:182-244` resolves
66	  `SLUG_TO_LEGACY_TASK["drb_72_ai_labor"] == 72` from legacy `query.jsonl`.
67	- Gate-B override: `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:5706-5731` selects legacy and attaches the
68	  legacy-only `question_lineage` marker.
69	- Direct task-72 sweep override: `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:21946-21970` does the same.
70	- Scope protocol: `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9905-9912` passes `q["question"]`, and
71	  `src/polaris_graph/nodes/scope_gate.py:1131-1138` stores it as `protocol.research_question`.
72	- Retrieval is rooted in `_clean_question`, initialized from `q["question"]` at
73	  `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9718` and passed to the active FS/IterResearch lane at
74	  `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:10899-10912`.
75	- V30 compile/frame and contract plans receive `q["question"]` at
76	  `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:13887-13901` and `:14269-14286`.
77	- Both generator/re-entry paths receive it at `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:16022-16035`.
78	- H1 uses it at `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:17735-17737`.
79	- Corpus snapshot stores it and the legacy marker at
80	  `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:15847-15860`.
81	- RACE scorer reads the answered question/slug/lineage and invokes the legacy split-brain guard
82	  before pack writes at `scripts/score_report_race.py:69-107`.
83	- All assignment scans found no later idx-56 rewrite outside the two intended override blocks.
84	- The raw registered task-72 question, legacy resolver, and `query.jsonl` id 72 are in fact
85	  byte-equal and share SHA
86	  `c598a9cf1912e8932da930682bccbce02157df4430cfd86c7f8303078d918fbf`.
87	- The three output-contract consumers all route through the lineage-aware loader:
88	  pre-spend at `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:4528-4529`, render at
89	  `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:18173-18195`, and post-render at
90	  `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_validity_gate.py:438-461`. Legacy receives `None`.
91	
92	Blocking incompleteness:
93	
94	- `gate0_lineage.assert_drb_slug_registered` is not lineage-aware. It accepts any slug in
95	  `DRB_SLUGS_WITHOUT_CANONICAL_GOLD` at `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:116-127`.
96	- The direct sweep's no-mapping legacy rejection is restricted to `_slug in SLUG_TO_IDX` at
97	  `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:21971-21982`. Therefore an explicitly registered no-gold
98	  benchmark slug such as `drb_90_adas_liability` is a benchmark, has no legacy mapping, passes
99	  registration, misses both legacy branches, and is appended unchanged at `:21991`. This violates
100	  the v2 requirement that every legacy/benchmark-slug pair without a mapping fail loud and makes
101	  global/`--only drb_90...` selector behavior split from Gate-B, whose broader
102	  `is_benchmark_slug` rejection at `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:5732-5741` is correct.
103	
104	Required fix:
105	
106	- Make legacy registration/checking reject **every** benchmark slug absent from
107	  `SLUG_TO_LEGACY_TASK`, including explicit no-gold slugs, in the shared resolver/registration
108	  seam; use that same check from both Gate-B and direct sweep.
109	
110	### 3. FAIL-LOUD PRESERVATION — FAIL
111	
112	PASS portions:
113	
114	- Unknown selectors fail at `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:64-77`.
115	- Canonical resolver legacy/no-mapping fails at `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:230-238`.
116	- Gate-B rejects every legacy benchmark slug with no mapping at
117	  `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:5732-5741`.
118	- Packed/answered/canonical mismatches fail via the SHA guard at
119	  `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:274-302`.
120	- The scorer refuses missing snapshot evidence or wrong stored lineage at
121	  `scripts/score_report_race.py:69-98`.
122	
123	FAIL portions:
124	
125	- Direct sweep's explicit-no-gold hole is described in item 2.
126	- “Raw registered question equals legacy canonical” is not a raw assertion:
127	  `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:5716-5717` and
128	  `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:21963-21964` compare only `sha256_text`.
129	  `sha256_text` normalizes all whitespace first at
130	  `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:144-151`.
131	- The new test `tests/dr_benchmark/test_stage0_lineage_seam.py:197-206` explicitly passes three
132	  raw-different strings (`"LEGACY   Q"`, `"LEGACY Q"`, `"LEGACY\nQ"`), contradicting the required
133	  raw-AND-SHA test. The task-72 integration test at
134	  `tests/dr_benchmark/test_stage0_lineage_sweep_integration.py:24-37` asserts only SHA equality.
135	
136	Required fix:
137	
138	- Assert raw string/byte equality **and** normalized SHA equality for the registered legacy
139	  question and the packed/answered/canonical evidence where v2 requires both.
140	- Replace the whitespace-drift acceptance test with explicit raw-equality plus SHA-equality tests.
141	
142	### 4. COVERAGE GATE REPORT-ONLY FOR LEGACY — FAIL
143	
144	The code changed at `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:1912-1939` is
145	`_required_entity_ledger_failed_under_strict`: it controls only whether an **exception while
146	building/rendering the RequiredEntityLedger** becomes the F27 post-spend abort at
147	`scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:20824-20845`.
148	
149	That is not the native required-element coverage decision identified by v2:
150	
151	- native denominator load: `src/polaris_graph/roles/native_gate_b_inputs.py:283-301`;
152	- fixed denominator enters `CoverageLedger` at
153	  `src/polaris_graph/roles/native_gate_b_inputs.py:959-960,1228-1231`;
154	- actual below-threshold coverage HOLD is created at
155	  `src/polaris_graph/roles/release_policy.py:235-254`.
156	
157	Consequences:
158	
159	- the intended native coverage result has not been made lineage-aware at its severity/disposition
160	  seam;
161	- if the separate report-level ledger implementation throws, legacy now suppresses the F27 abort
162	  but keeps only the WARN at `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:20464-20471`; it produces neither the
163	  required coverage report nor a durable manifest failure marker. That is log-only fail-soft, not a
164	  report-only coverage decision;
165	- the new tests at `tests/dr_benchmark/test_stage0_lineage_seam.py:284-293` and
166	  `test_stage0_lineage_sweep_integration.py:54-64` test this wrong exception predicate, not a native
167	  low-coverage result.
168	
169	This edit is not a content lever—it changes status severity only—but it is the wrong severity
170	surface and can hide a broken coverage-report producer.
171	
172	Required fix:
173	
174	- Preserve the native coverage fraction/gaps/audit unchanged, and make only the idx-56
175	  required-element **coverage-shortfall blocker** report-only for `legacy_race_task` at an outer
176	  lineage-aware disposition seam. Do not relax claim faithfulness, S0/fabrication/zero-grounding
177	  decisions, or edit content.
178	- Keep a RequiredEntityLedger implementation failure fail-loud/durably disclosed; do not silently
179	  convert it to a WARN-only success.
180	- Add a behavioral test that supplies a below-threshold native fixed-denominator result and proves:
181	  default blocks as before, legacy retains the exact coverage telemetry/gap but does not abort for
182	  that reason alone.
183	
184	### 5. GHOST AUDIT — PASS
185	
186	Exact GHOST_BAN regex over `git diff -- scripts/ src/` produced four hits:
187	
188	1. `binding by idx` — identity-lineage description;
189	2. `Fail CLOSED` — pre-existing adjacent model-selector comment;
190	3. `touches NO faithfulness gate (... NLI ...)` — explicit exclusion;
191	4. `NO binding` — explanation of the historical unbound-question failure.
192	
193	None is a proposal for admission, entailment/NLI, premise binding, suppression, or
194	post-generation content acceptance.
195	
196	Frozen-module diff is empty for:
197	
198	- `src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py`
199	- `src/polaris_graph/clinical_generator/strict_verify.py`
200	
201	Structural checks:
202	
203	- (a) PASS — no emitted-vs-admitted/planned comparison;
204	- (b) PASS — no new producer-to-render content-drop/replace predicate;
205	- (c) PASS — no new import/change of either frozen faithfulness module;
206	- (d) PASS for content checks — production checks are lineage identity/pre-spend guards, not
207	  content admission; deterministic content ship checks remain tests only;
208	- (e) PASS — no new dataclass/type or banned carrier fields.
209	
210	`SLUG_TO_LEGACY_TASK` contains task 72 only as the required canonical identity registry; no
211	task-72 domain vocabulary, prompt lever, target count, or score-forcing rule was added.
212	
213	### 6. BUG / OFF-BY-SEAM / MISSED OVERRIDE — FAIL
214	
215	Blocking bugs are the direct no-gold fail-open, wrong coverage-severity seam, and missing raw
216	equality above.
217	
218	Additional required lineage fix:
219	
220	- `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9554-9559` passes
221	  `expected_lineage=q.get("question_lineage")`. Default queries have no marker, so this is `None`,
222	  and `src/polaris_graph/generator/corpus_snapshot.py:198-207` performs no lineage check.
223	  Consequently a stored legacy snapshot is accepted by the loader when a later run selects the
224	  default lineage. The following question-SHA guard at `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9537-9560`
225	  catches task 72 today because its legacy and idx-56 questions differ, but the explicit invariant
226	  “resume rejects a mismatched stored lineage” is only enforced default-to-legacy, not
227	  legacy-to-default.
228	- `tests/dr_benchmark/test_stage0_lineage_seam.py:263-278` exposes this gap: it deliberately calls
229	  `expected_lineage=None` as a no-check, while the only legacy-to-default rejection test calls the
230	  loader manually with explicit `drb_ii_idx`; the production caller never does that.
231	
232	Required fix:
233	
234	- At the production resume seam, treat an absent query marker as the effective
235	  `drb_ii_idx` lineage for comparison while continuing to omit the default lineage from serialized
236	  artifacts. Add caller-level tests for both mismatch directions.
237	
238	Test-harness incompleteness:
239	
240	- `tests/dr_benchmark/test_stage0_lineage_sweep_integration.py:67-79` counts alias call sites. It
241	  can miss a second override written through another alias/direct loader and does not prove any
242	  downstream value.
243	- No new test behaviorally asserts the protocol, retrieval seed, compile frame, contract plan,
244	  generator argument, H1, snapshot, and scorer pack all carry the same task-72 raw value/SHA.
245	- No scorer guard test is present.
246	- Default “golden identity” tests check manifest shape/no snapshot key rather than comparing exact
247	  HEAD bytes; they do not exercise selected `q` or protocol bytes.
248	
249	## Verification evidence and limitations
250	
251	- All six modified production files and both new tests compile successfully via Python `compile()`.
252	- `git diff --check` reports CR-at-EOL on added `run_gate_b.py` lines because that entire file is
253	  already CRLF in both HEAD and worktree (HEAD: 6,859/6,859 CRLF; worktree: 6,922/6,922 CRLF);
254	  no mixed-newline regression was found.
255	- The requested pytest files could not be executed in this checkout: `pytest` is not installed,
256	  `python3 -m pytest` reports `No module named pytest`, no project virtualenv is present, and importing
257	  the full sweep directly also lacks `python-dotenv`. No dependency/network installation was
258	  attempted (operator required no web). The pure deterministic harnesses listed in item 1 were run
259	  with stdlib only.
260	- Two additional untracked shell files (`scripts/run_race_batch3_max.sh`,
261	  `scripts/run_race_max_focus.sh`) are present in the shared worktree but are outside the stated
262	  six-file diff and were not treated as part of this Stage-0 review.
263	- The binding campaign `state/beatboth_campaign/loop_state.json` referenced by `AGENTS.md` is absent
264	  from this worktree. The operator's explicit bounded read-only Stage-0 authorization was used.
265	
266	## Single most important risk
267	
268	**The implementation claims the legacy required-element coverage gate is report-only, but it
269	actually disables only the post-spend abort for a broken coverage-ledger reporter.** That can both
270	leave the real native idx-56 coverage disposition unaddressed and allow a legacy success with the
271	coverage disclosure silently absent. The re-baseline would then spend against a seam whose
272	ship-decision test is testing the wrong predicate.

# Stage-0 lineage seam — Sol diff-gate verdict

## Verdict

**NO-GO to commit and NO-GO to run the re-baseline.**

The default `drb_ii_idx` path is clean and the mapped task-72 legacy happy path is mostly
single-brained, but the implementation does not fully match the reconciled v2 design:

1. the direct-sweep legacy branch fails open for an explicitly registered no-gold benchmark slug
   with no legacy mapping;
2. the purported legacy “coverage report-only” change alters the wrong predicate (a
   RequiredEntityLedger implementation-error abort), not the native fixed-denominator
   required-element coverage decision;
3. raw-byte equality is not enforced/tested as required; the production comparisons use only a
   SHA over whitespace-normalized text, and a new test explicitly accepts raw drift;
4. the sweep caller does not enforce the snapshot lineage mismatch in the legacy-to-default
   direction (the question-SHA guard happens to catch task-72 because the two questions differ,
   but the required lineage invariant is incomplete);
5. the new tests do not behaviorally exercise all advertised seams or the scorer.

No generation or scoring spend should start until these are fixed and re-diff-gated.

## Per-item results

### 1. DEFAULT-PATH BYTE IDENTITY — PASS

Grounded code review:

- `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:215-244` keeps the positional
  `(slug, tasks_path)` call valid and selects the unchanged DRB-II loader on the default branch.
- `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:5688` still force-assigns
  `PG_BENCHMARK_OFFICIAL_QUESTION="1"`.
- `_BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_REQUIRED_FLAGS` still contains that flag exactly once at
  `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:2064-2070`; the selector is not added to the tuple.
- The selector allowlist at `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:4824-4842` is inert when unset and
  accepts explicit `drb_ii_idx`.
- The default Gate-B override remains the old canonical-idx branch at
  `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:5742-5750`; it adds no lineage key.
- `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_validity_gate.py:128-144` loads the normal output contract for unset
  or `drb_ii_idx`.
- `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:15847-15860` passes `lineage=None` on the default path, and
  `src/polaris_graph/generator/corpus_snapshot.py:134-137` therefore adds no snapshot key.
- `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:351-374` preserves the default manifest shape and key
  order, including `canonical_idx`, with no `lineage` key.
- `scripts/score_report_race.py:64-69` adds only a stdlib gate0 import/env read on default; the
  legacy scoring block is not entered.

Read-only deterministic HEAD-vs-worktree harness results:

- default canonical resolver output: exact;
- default lineage-manifest dict and serialized JSON: exact;
- default snapshot bytes: exact, with no `lineage` key;
- output-contract result: exact for unset and explicit `drb_ii_idx`;
- required-flags tuple: exact;
- default coverage-helper truth table: exact for unset and explicit `drb_ii_idx`;
- default resolver opened only the DRB-II task file and did not open `query.jsonl`.

No default-path artifact or behavior divergence was found for the stated surfaces.

### 2. SINGLE-BRAIN COMPLETENESS FOR LEGACY — FAIL

What is correct for mapped task 72:

- Resolver: `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:182-244` resolves
  `SLUG_TO_LEGACY_TASK["drb_72_ai_labor"] == 72` from legacy `query.jsonl`.
- Gate-B override: `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:5706-5731` selects legacy and attaches the
  legacy-only `question_lineage` marker.
- Direct task-72 sweep override: `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:21946-21970` does the same.
- Scope protocol: `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9905-9912` passes `q["question"]`, and
  `src/polaris_graph/nodes/scope_gate.py:1131-1138` stores it as `protocol.research_question`.
- Retrieval is rooted in `_clean_question`, initialized from `q["question"]` at
  `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9718` and passed to the active FS/IterResearch lane at
  `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:10899-10912`.
- V30 compile/frame and contract plans receive `q["question"]` at
  `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:13887-13901` and `:14269-14286`.
- Both generator/re-entry paths receive it at `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:16022-16035`.
- H1 uses it at `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:17735-17737`.
- Corpus snapshot stores it and the legacy marker at
  `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:15847-15860`.
- RACE scorer reads the answered question/slug/lineage and invokes the legacy split-brain guard
  before pack writes at `scripts/score_report_race.py:69-107`.
- All assignment scans found no later idx-56 rewrite outside the two intended override blocks.
- The raw registered task-72 question, legacy resolver, and `query.jsonl` id 72 are in fact
  byte-equal and share SHA
  `c598a9cf1912e8932da930682bccbce02157df4430cfd86c7f8303078d918fbf`.
- The three output-contract consumers all route through the lineage-aware loader:
  pre-spend at `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:4528-4529`, render at
  `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:18173-18195`, and post-render at
  `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_validity_gate.py:438-461`. Legacy receives `None`.

Blocking incompleteness:

- `gate0_lineage.assert_drb_slug_registered` is not lineage-aware. It accepts any slug in
  `DRB_SLUGS_WITHOUT_CANONICAL_GOLD` at `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:116-127`.
- The direct sweep's no-mapping legacy rejection is restricted to `_slug in SLUG_TO_IDX` at
  `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:21971-21982`. Therefore an explicitly registered no-gold
  benchmark slug such as `drb_90_adas_liability` is a benchmark, has no legacy mapping, passes
  registration, misses both legacy branches, and is appended unchanged at `:21991`. This violates
  the v2 requirement that every legacy/benchmark-slug pair without a mapping fail loud and makes
  global/`--only drb_90...` selector behavior split from Gate-B, whose broader
  `is_benchmark_slug` rejection at `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:5732-5741` is correct.

Required fix:

- Make legacy registration/checking reject **every** benchmark slug absent from
  `SLUG_TO_LEGACY_TASK`, including explicit no-gold slugs, in the shared resolver/registration
  seam; use that same check from both Gate-B and direct sweep.

### 3. FAIL-LOUD PRESERVATION — FAIL

PASS portions:

- Unknown selectors fail at `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:64-77`.
- Canonical resolver legacy/no-mapping fails at `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:230-238`.
- Gate-B rejects every legacy benchmark slug with no mapping at
  `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:5732-5741`.
- Packed/answered/canonical mismatches fail via the SHA guard at
  `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:274-302`.
- The scorer refuses missing snapshot evidence or wrong stored lineage at
  `scripts/score_report_race.py:69-98`.

FAIL portions:

- Direct sweep's explicit-no-gold hole is described in item 2.
- “Raw registered question equals legacy canonical” is not a raw assertion:
  `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:5716-5717` and
  `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:21963-21964` compare only `sha256_text`.
  `sha256_text` normalizes all whitespace first at
  `scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py:144-151`.
- The new test `tests/dr_benchmark/test_stage0_lineage_seam.py:197-206` explicitly passes three
  raw-different strings (`"LEGACY   Q"`, `"LEGACY Q"`, `"LEGACY\nQ"`), contradicting the required
  raw-AND-SHA test. The task-72 integration test at
  `tests/dr_benchmark/test_stage0_lineage_sweep_integration.py:24-37` asserts only SHA equality.

Required fix:

- Assert raw string/byte equality **and** normalized SHA equality for the registered legacy
  question and the packed/answered/canonical evidence where v2 requires both.
- Replace the whitespace-drift acceptance test with explicit raw-equality plus SHA-equality tests.

### 4. COVERAGE GATE REPORT-ONLY FOR LEGACY — FAIL

The code changed at `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:1912-1939` is
`_required_entity_ledger_failed_under_strict`: it controls only whether an **exception while
building/rendering the RequiredEntityLedger** becomes the F27 post-spend abort at
`scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:20824-20845`.

That is not the native required-element coverage decision identified by v2:

- native denominator load: `src/polaris_graph/roles/native_gate_b_inputs.py:283-301`;
- fixed denominator enters `CoverageLedger` at
  `src/polaris_graph/roles/native_gate_b_inputs.py:959-960,1228-1231`;
- actual below-threshold coverage HOLD is created at
  `src/polaris_graph/roles/release_policy.py:235-254`.

Consequences:

- the intended native coverage result has not been made lineage-aware at its severity/disposition
  seam;
- if the separate report-level ledger implementation throws, legacy now suppresses the F27 abort
  but keeps only the WARN at `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:20464-20471`; it produces neither the
  required coverage report nor a durable manifest failure marker. That is log-only fail-soft, not a
  report-only coverage decision;
- the new tests at `tests/dr_benchmark/test_stage0_lineage_seam.py:284-293` and
  `test_stage0_lineage_sweep_integration.py:54-64` test this wrong exception predicate, not a native
  low-coverage result.

This edit is not a content lever—it changes status severity only—but it is the wrong severity
surface and can hide a broken coverage-report producer.

Required fix:

- Preserve the native coverage fraction/gaps/audit unchanged, and make only the idx-56
  required-element **coverage-shortfall blocker** report-only for `legacy_race_task` at an outer
  lineage-aware disposition seam. Do not relax claim faithfulness, S0/fabrication/zero-grounding
  decisions, or edit content.
- Keep a RequiredEntityLedger implementation failure fail-loud/durably disclosed; do not silently
  convert it to a WARN-only success.
- Add a behavioral test that supplies a below-threshold native fixed-denominator result and proves:
  default blocks as before, legacy retains the exact coverage telemetry/gap but does not abort for
  that reason alone.

### 5. GHOST AUDIT — PASS

Exact GHOST_BAN regex over `git diff -- scripts/ src/` produced four hits:

1. `binding by idx` — identity-lineage description;
2. `Fail CLOSED` — pre-existing adjacent model-selector comment;
3. `touches NO faithfulness gate (... NLI ...)` — explicit exclusion;
4. `NO binding` — explanation of the historical unbound-question failure.

None is a proposal for admission, entailment/NLI, premise binding, suppression, or
post-generation content acceptance.

Frozen-module diff is empty for:

- `src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py`
- `src/polaris_graph/clinical_generator/strict_verify.py`

Structural checks:

- (a) PASS — no emitted-vs-admitted/planned comparison;
- (b) PASS — no new producer-to-render content-drop/replace predicate;
- (c) PASS — no new import/change of either frozen faithfulness module;
- (d) PASS for content checks — production checks are lineage identity/pre-spend guards, not
  content admission; deterministic content ship checks remain tests only;
- (e) PASS — no new dataclass/type or banned carrier fields.

`SLUG_TO_LEGACY_TASK` contains task 72 only as the required canonical identity registry; no
task-72 domain vocabulary, prompt lever, target count, or score-forcing rule was added.

### 6. BUG / OFF-BY-SEAM / MISSED OVERRIDE — FAIL

Blocking bugs are the direct no-gold fail-open, wrong coverage-severity seam, and missing raw
equality above.

Additional required lineage fix:

- `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9554-9559` passes
  `expected_lineage=q.get("question_lineage")`. Default queries have no marker, so this is `None`,
  and `src/polaris_graph/generator/corpus_snapshot.py:198-207` performs no lineage check.
  Consequently a stored legacy snapshot is accepted by the loader when a later run selects the
  default lineage. The following question-SHA guard at `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9537-9560`
  catches task 72 today because its legacy and idx-56 questions differ, but the explicit invariant
  “resume rejects a mismatched stored lineage” is only enforced default-to-legacy, not
  legacy-to-default.
- `tests/dr_benchmark/test_stage0_lineage_seam.py:263-278` exposes this gap: it deliberately calls
  `expected_lineage=None` as a no-check, while the only legacy-to-default rejection test calls the
  loader manually with explicit `drb_ii_idx`; the production caller never does that.

Required fix:

- At the production resume seam, treat an absent query marker as the effective
  `drb_ii_idx` lineage for comparison while continuing to omit the default lineage from serialized
  artifacts. Add caller-level tests for both mismatch directions.

Test-harness incompleteness:

- `tests/dr_benchmark/test_stage0_lineage_sweep_integration.py:67-79` counts alias call sites. It
  can miss a second override written through another alias/direct loader and does not prove any
  downstream value.
- No new test behaviorally asserts the protocol, retrieval seed, compile frame, contract plan,
  generator argument, H1, snapshot, and scorer pack all carry the same task-72 raw value/SHA.
- No scorer guard test is present.
- Default “golden identity” tests check manifest shape/no snapshot key rather than comparing exact
  HEAD bytes; they do not exercise selected `q` or protocol bytes.

## Verification evidence and limitations

- All six modified production files and both new tests compile successfully via Python `compile()`.
- `git diff --check` reports CR-at-EOL on added `run_gate_b.py` lines because that entire file is
  already CRLF in both HEAD and worktree (HEAD: 6,859/6,859 CRLF; worktree: 6,922/6,922 CRLF);
  no mixed-newline regression was found.
- The requested pytest files could not be executed in this checkout: `pytest` is not installed,
  `python3 -m pytest` reports `No module named pytest`, no project virtualenv is present, and importing
  the full sweep directly also lacks `python-dotenv`. No dependency/network installation was
  attempted (operator required no web). The pure deterministic harnesses listed in item 1 were run
  with stdlib only.
- Two additional untracked shell files (`scripts/run_race_batch3_max.sh`,
  `scripts/run_race_max_focus.sh`) are present in the shared worktree but are outside the stated
  six-file diff and were not treated as part of this Stage-0 review.
- The binding campaign `state/beatboth_campaign/loop_state.json` referenced by `AGENTS.md` is absent
  from this worktree. The operator's explicit bounded read-only Stage-0 authorization was used.

## Single most important risk

**The implementation claims the legacy required-element coverage gate is report-only, but it
actually disables only the post-spend abort for a broken coverage-ledger reporter.** That can both
leave the real native idx-56 coverage disposition unaddressed and allow a legacy success with the
coverage disclosure silently absent. The re-baseline would then spend against a seam whose
ship-decision test is testing the wrong predicate.

2	
3	Sol NO-GO'd v1 as incomplete; Fable GO-WITH-CHANGES. They converge: the harness is wired for DRB-II idx-56 on the
4	`drb_72_ai_labor` slug in ~9 seams; ALL must become lineage-aware or the run is split-brained and fail-louds
5	post-spend. This v2 is the union of both edit lists. Pure lineage/identity plumbing — NO content lever, NO ghost

8	## Design (both agree)
9	- Selector `PG_BENCHMARK_QUESTION_LINEAGE`: read with an **in-process default** `drb_ii_idx` (do NOT write it to
10	  env; default serialized artifacts gain no key); `legacy_race_task` = the task-72 RACE lineage.
11	- **KEEP the forced `PG_BENCHMARK_OFFICIAL_QUESTION="1"` + its preflight required-flag tuple BYTE-IDENTICAL** (Sol

   fails for a legacy/slug pair with no legacy mapping (`:56-78`). Keep positional `(slug, tasks_path)` valid.
2. **run_gate_b override** (`run_gate_b.py:5645-5706`): default = current idx override. Legacy = **assert the raw

   (`run_gate_b.py:4511-4548`), summary-table renderer headers (`run_honest_sweep_r3.py:18144-18165`), and
   post-render validity gate (`run_validity_gate.py:111-129,423-458`). idx-56's `intent_anchors ["generative ai"]`
   would FAIL-LOUD a legacy run AFTER full spend + inject the 5-column table. **Resolution: make
   `load_task_output_contract` lineage-aware; legacy → `None` (the existing documented no-op).** One gate covers all
   three consumers.
6. **corpus_snapshot** (`run_honest_sweep_r3.py:15818-15838`; `corpus_snapshot.py:90-126`): add a legacy-only
   lineage field; **resume rejects a mismatched stored lineage** (needed for the 3-draw frozen-corpus resume).
   Existing question-SHA guards `:9517-9537`, `:15910-15919` stay.
7. **Scorer** (`score_report_race.py:44-68`): legacy-selector-gated call to the lineage-aware `assert_no_split_brain`
   (read answered question + slug from the run snapshot/manifest) before writing scores; fail loud if the answered
   evidence is missing. Default scorer path unchanged.
8. **Question flows through** (verify single-brain, no second override): scope_gate protocol.research_question
   (`scope_gate.py:872-884,1131-1169`), retrieval seed (`run_honest_sweep_r3.py:9822-9877,10902-10920`), V30
   compile_frame + contract-plan question + generator (`:13858-13884,14252-14269,16012-16016`), H1
   (`:17713-17715,17853-17869`).
9. **V30/D8 per-query contract (the ONE judgment call — flag for diff-gate)** (`workforce.yaml:161-365`;
   `native_gate_b_inputs.py:283-301,943-960`): workforce.yaml was authored FOR idx-56, shapes the V30 outline/entity
   structure AND supplies the fail-closed Gate-B required-element COVERAGE denominator. task-72 and idx-56 are the
   SAME topic (AI labor restructuring), so keeping the workforce contract STRUCTURE is acceptable (the report still
   answers task-72 in prose, scored by RACE task-72). **The risk is only the fail-CLOSED coverage gate aborting a
   task-72 run.** **Resolution (Opus call, flag for both diff-gaters): for `legacy_race_task`, make the DRB-II
   required-element coverage gate REPORT-ONLY (non-fatal), since we score on RACE task-72, not the idx-56 coverage
   rubric.** No content change; a lineage-aware gate-severity change. If either diff-gater rejects this, fall back
   to authoring a task-72 per-query contract (larger) — but do not ship a run that can abort post-spend.

## TESTS (deterministic; ship-decision only)
- Default off-state GOLDEN identity over: env mutations, selected `q`, canonical-file opens (no query.jsonl read on
  default), protocol bytes, snapshot bytes, output-contract resolution, manifest — all byte-identical to HEAD.
- Legacy: answered-question sha == query.jsonl id=72 at every seam (launch, protocol, retrieval seed, compile_frame,
  contract plan, H1, snapshot, scorer pack); NO DRB-II gold read; output-contract = None; coverage gate non-fatal.
- Split-brain guard still FAILS LOUD on a deliberate packed/answered mismatch (raw AND sha256 equality).
- Unregistered-slug + legacy/slug-with-no-mapping FAIL-LOUD preserved.
- `git diff` clean on provenance_generator/strict_verify; GHOST_BAN grep clean (identity/lineage only).

## BUILD ORDER (seam-by-seam, then ONE both-model diff-gate over the whole diff)
(1) gate0_lineage resolver+registry+manifest → (2) run_gate_b override+preflight allowlist → (5) output-contract
lineage gate → (4) direct sweep override → (6) corpus_snapshot lineage → (7) scorer guard → (9) coverage-gate
severity → (8) verify flow single-brain via tests. Then both-model diff-gate + ghost-audit BEFORE the re-baseline.

1	# GHOST_BAN — standing guard against overfit / faithfulness-ghost / post-generation-fix
2	
3	Prepended to EVERY Sol/Fable brief. Run by Opus as a mechanical audit on EVERY verdict, spec, and code diff BEFORE
4	acceptance. Origin: the Wave-1 build (Sol-solo gates v1–v4) drifted into the banned apparatus over five rounds; the
5	operator caught it and ordered a rollback (MASTER_ACTION_PLAN §12). This guard exists so it never recurs.
6	
7	## THE THREE BANNED FAMILIES (any design that does these is REJECTED)
8	
9	### 1. OVERFIT
10	- task/domain literals in runtime code/prompts/config: task-72 domain nouns (labor / AI / 4IR / industry / journal
11	  names), benchmark task IDs, venue whitelists, sector lists, domain-vocabulary lookup tables.
12	- magic counts / hardcoded numbers that force a score / breadth / paragraph / source / section / table / citation
13	  target.
14	- adjective or version flag names (max / full / rich / smart / deep / enhanced / better; "wave1"; version numbers).
15	- a fix that only passes on task-72. Every fix must pass its deterministic TEST on held-out tasks 91 / 100 / 73.
16	
17	### 2. FAITHFULNESS GHOST
18	- entailment / NLI machinery of ANY kind (model, predicate, or "does the prose entail X" runtime check).
19	- runtime admission gate: "licensed-inference admission", "premise-relation license", "operator-license
20	  predicate", "canary that rejects a sentence before/after the producer".
21	- re-implementing, importing, modifying, weakening, or wrapping the faithfulness engine
22	  (`provenance_generator.py`, `clinical_generator/strict_verify.py`).
23	- any second verification / acceptance layer on top of the EXISTING per-sentence verifier.
24	- typed carriers whose fields include premise IDs, admitted tokens, marker bindings, or reasoning operators.
25	
26	### 3. POST-GENERATION FIX
27	- editing / rewriting / dropping / re-ranking / suppressing any sentence or the report AFTER generation.
28	- "exact emitted==admitted binding audit", "non-scoreable-on-mismatch", "release_allowed=False" on content grounds,
29	  "fail-closed" content states, per-route acceptance checks woven into the generation call path.
30	- post-assembly content insertion (summary-table-compose style).
31	- SOLE EXCEPTION: layout-only whitespace/heading normalization that PROVABLY preserves content (ordered
32	  non-whitespace token stream + citation markers byte-identical before/after; no word/number/marker/sentence
33	  change), with its token-identity test attached.
34	
35	## THE POSITIVE RULE (what a lever MAY be)
36	- a PRE-GENERATION change to prompt text / outline-or-plan text / plan-merge role selection / retrieval scope that
37	  executes BEFORE the producer LLM call it shapes, and reaches the ACTIVE writer.
38	- rely on the EXISTING faithfulness engine, untouched. Verifier-strip is lawful: if it drops the new sentence, the
39	  responses are better upstream evidence ownership OR drop the lever — never rescue / re-insert / relax.
40	- deterministic checks are TESTS that decide whether a lever SHIPS (scripts under `tests/` or standalone lints run
41	  on emitted artifacts) — nothing in the generation call path reads their result.
42	- a shared PRE-GENERATION plan structure is allowed ONLY if it (a) exists solely before generation, (b) renders
43	  into prompt/plan text then has no runtime reader, (c) carries no admission/binding/entailment fields, (d) is
44	  never compared against emitted text. If it pulls toward per-sentence identity / premises / admitted candidates →
45	  use the per-lever prompt change instead, or DROP THE LEVER.
46	- **DROP-LEVER DEFAULT:** if a gap cannot be fixed by a clean pre-generation change verified by the existing
47	  engine, DROP it and record it dropped. Never invent machinery to rescue a lever.
48	
49	## MECHANICAL AUDIT (Opus runs this on every verdict / spec / diff; reviewer runs it on every wave diff)
50	```
51	grep -inE 'admission|canary|entail|nli|licens|binding|premise|operator[_ -]?licen|non[_ -]?scoreable|scoreab|suppress|fail[_ -]?closed|admitted|emitted[_ =]|reasoning operator|analyticalcontract' <target>
52	```
53	Every hit must be a REJECTION / naming-to-exclude / a reference to this ban — NEVER a proposal. Any proposing hit
54	inside `src/` or `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py` / `scripts/dr_benchmark/` = REJECTED.
55	PLUS five structural checks (reject on any one failure):
56	- (a) no new code path compares emitted text against a stored "admitted/planned" text;
57	- (b) no new predicate consulted between any producer call and final render that can drop or replace content
58	  (layout normalizer excepted, with its token-identity test attached);
59	- (c) no new import of either frozen faithfulness module;
60	- (d) every new deterministic check lives under `tests/` or a standalone lint; nothing in the generation call path
61	  reads its result;
62	- (e) no new dataclass/type whose fields include premise IDs, admitted tokens, marker bindings, or reasoning
63	  operators.
64	
65	## PROCESS GUARD
66	- TWO-MODEL always: every design AND every build diff is gated by BOTH Fable and Sol. NEVER a single-model gate.
67	  (The drift happened under solo-Sol; Fable authored "no entailment machinery" and is the counterweight.)
68	- Opus holds the ban over Sol's correctness-maximizing: if Sol proposes an admission/entailment/binding/post-gen
69	  apparatus "for correctness", Opus REJECTS it and applies the clean form or drops the lever — never gate-obeys.
70

# GHOST_BAN — standing guard against overfit / faithfulness-ghost / post-generation-fix

Prepended to EVERY Sol/Fable brief. Run by Opus as a mechanical audit on EVERY verdict, spec, and code diff BEFORE
acceptance. Origin: the Wave-1 build (Sol-solo gates v1–v4) drifted into the banned apparatus over five rounds; the
operator caught it and ordered a rollback (MASTER_ACTION_PLAN §12). This guard exists so it never recurs.

## THE THREE BANNED FAMILIES (any design that does these is REJECTED)

### 1. OVERFIT
- task/domain literals in runtime code/prompts/config: task-72 domain nouns (labor / AI / 4IR / industry / journal
  names), benchmark task IDs, venue whitelists, sector lists, domain-vocabulary lookup tables.
- magic counts / hardcoded numbers that force a score / breadth / paragraph / source / section / table / citation
  target.
- adjective or version flag names (max / full / rich / smart / deep / enhanced / better; "wave1"; version numbers).
- a fix that only passes on task-72. Every fix must pass its deterministic TEST on held-out tasks 91 / 100 / 73.

### 2. FAITHFULNESS GHOST
- entailment / NLI machinery of ANY kind (model, predicate, or "does the prose entail X" runtime check).
- runtime admission gate: "licensed-inference admission", "premise-relation license", "operator-license
  predicate", "canary that rejects a sentence before/after the producer".
- re-implementing, importing, modifying, weakening, or wrapping the faithfulness engine
  (`provenance_generator.py`, `clinical_generator/strict_verify.py`).
- any second verification / acceptance layer on top of the EXISTING per-sentence verifier.
- typed carriers whose fields include premise IDs, admitted tokens, marker bindings, or reasoning operators.

### 3. POST-GENERATION FIX
- editing / rewriting / dropping / re-ranking / suppressing any sentence or the report AFTER generation.
- "exact emitted==admitted binding audit", "non-scoreable-on-mismatch", "release_allowed=False" on content grounds,
  "fail-closed" content states, per-route acceptance checks woven into the generation call path.
- post-assembly content insertion (summary-table-compose style).
- SOLE EXCEPTION: layout-only whitespace/heading normalization that PROVABLY preserves content (ordered
  non-whitespace token stream + citation markers byte-identical before/after; no word/number/marker/sentence
  change), with its token-identity test attached.

## THE POSITIVE RULE (what a lever MAY be)
- a PRE-GENERATION change to prompt text / outline-or-plan text / plan-merge role selection / retrieval scope that
  executes BEFORE the producer LLM call it shapes, and reaches the ACTIVE writer.
- rely on the EXISTING faithfulness engine, untouched. Verifier-strip is lawful: if it drops the new sentence, the
  responses are better upstream evidence ownership OR drop the lever — never rescue / re-insert / relax.
- deterministic checks are TESTS that decide whether a lever SHIPS (scripts under `tests/` or standalone lints run
  on emitted artifacts) — nothing in the generation call path reads their result.
- a shared PRE-GENERATION plan structure is allowed ONLY if it (a) exists solely before generation, (b) renders
  into prompt/plan text then has no runtime reader, (c) carries no admission/binding/entailment fields, (d) is
  never compared against emitted text. If it pulls toward per-sentence identity / premises / admitted candidates →
  use the per-lever prompt change instead, or DROP THE LEVER.
- **DROP-LEVER DEFAULT:** if a gap cannot be fixed by a clean pre-generation change verified by the existing
  engine, DROP it and record it dropped. Never invent machinery to rescue a lever.

## MECHANICAL AUDIT (Opus runs this on every verdict / spec / diff; reviewer runs it on every wave diff)
```
grep -inE 'admission|canary|entail|nli|licens|binding|premise|operator[_ -]?licen|non[_ -]?scoreable|scoreab|suppress|fail[_ -]?closed|admitted|emitted[_ =]|reasoning operator|analyticalcontract' <target>
```
Every hit must be a REJECTION / naming-to-exclude / a reference to this ban — NEVER a proposal. Any proposing hit
inside `src/` or `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py` / `scripts/dr_benchmark/` = REJECTED.
PLUS five structural checks (reject on any one failure):
- (a) no new code path compares emitted text against a stored "admitted/planned" text;
- (b) no new predicate consulted between any producer call and final render that can drop or replace content
  (layout normalizer excepted, with its token-identity test attached);
- (c) no new import of either frozen faithfulness module;
- (d) every new deterministic check lives under `tests/` or a standalone lint; nothing in the generation call path
  reads its result;
- (e) no new dataclass/type whose fields include premise IDs, admitted tokens, marker bindings, or reasoning
  operators.

## PROCESS GUARD
- TWO-MODEL always: every design AND every build diff is gated by BOTH Fable and Sol. NEVER a single-model gate.
  (The drift happened under solo-Sol; Fable authored "no entailment machinery" and is the counterweight.)
- Opus holds the ban over Sol's correctness-maximizing: if Sol proposes an admission/entailment/binding/post-gen
  apparatus "for correctness", Opus REJECTS it and applies the clean form or drops the lever — never gate-obeys.

content.content:
1	"""GATE 0 — artifact-lineage enforcement for the benchmark harness (I-qgen-001, GH #1291).
2	
3	The drb_72 disaster had two harness-integrity failures that produced trustworthy-looking
4	garbage scores:
5	  1. WRONG QUESTION: the sweep launched a hardcoded "Fourth Industrial Revolution" prompt
6	     that was NOT the canonical DeepResearch-Bench-II idx-56 question.
7	  2. SPLIT-BRAIN SCORING: the DeepTRACE/DRB pack used the canonical question while the report
8	     answered the substituted one.
9	
10	This module is the single source of truth that makes those impossible. It binds every
11	benchmark run to the canonical question by idx (no drifting hardcoded copies) and provides a
12	fail-loud equality check: launched == packed == answered == canonical. Nothing is hardcoded
13	that can silently drift from the gold file.
14	
15	No magic numbers, no silent fallbacks: a mismatch raises GateZeroLineageError (fail loud).
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import hashlib
21	import json
22	import os
23	import re
24	from dataclasses import dataclass
25	from typing import Any
26	
27	# Canonical gold file (DeepResearch-Bench-II tasks + rubrics).
28	DEFAULT_TASKS_PATH = os.path.join(
29	    "third_party", "DeepResearch-Bench-II", "tasks_and_rubrics.jsonl"
30	)
31	
32	# ── Question-lineage selector (Stage-0 lineage seam) ─────────────────────────
33	# ``PG_BENCHMARK_QUESTION_LINEAGE`` chooses WHICH canonical question a benchmark slug binds
34	# to. It is read with an IN-PROCESS DEFAULT (never written to env / never serialized) so the
35	# default path gains no artifact key and stays byte-for-byte identical to HEAD:
36	#   * ``drb_ii_idx``        (default) — today's behavior: bind to the DeepResearch-Bench-II
37	#                                        canonical question by idx (SLUG_TO_IDX + DEFAULT_TASKS_PATH).
38	#   * ``legacy_race_task``            — bind to the legacy DeepResearch-Bench (RACE) task question
39	#                                        (SLUG_TO_LEGACY_TASK id in query.jsonl), so a Gate-B/V30 run
40	#                                        answers the legacy task and is scored by score_report_race.py
41	#                                        --task-id <n>. NO content lever — only WHICH question text the
42	#                                        resolver returns changes; every faithfulness gate is untouched.
43	LINEAGE_SELECTOR_ENV = "PG_BENCHMARK_QUESTION_LINEAGE"
44	LINEAGE_DRB_II_IDX = "drb_ii_idx"          # in-process default (byte-identical to HEAD)
45	LINEAGE_LEGACY_RACE_TASK = "legacy_race_task"
46	ALLOWED_LINEAGES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({LINEAGE_DRB_II_IDX, LINEAGE_LEGACY_RACE_TASK})
47	
48	# slug -> legacy DeepResearch-Bench (RACE) task id, resolved from the legacy tasks file
49	# (``query.jsonl``, keyed by ``id``). Only slugs with an EXPLICIT legacy mapping may run under
50	# ``legacy_race_task`` — a legacy/slug pair with no entry FAILS LOUD (never a guessed id). The
51	# legacy id is the RACE ``--task-id`` (task-72), NOT the DRB-II idx (56); the two are different
52	# lineages by construction — a legacy run must NEVER be labelled ``canonical_idx=56``.
53	SLUG_TO_LEGACY_TASK: dict[str, int] = {
54	    "drb_72_ai_labor": 72,
55	}
56	
57	# Legacy tasks file (the DeepResearch-Bench RACE ``query.jsonl``, keyed by ``id`` + ``prompt``).
58	# Resolved through the ``third_party/deep_research_bench`` symlink; fail-loud if absent.
59	DEFAULT_LEGACY_TASKS_PATH = os.path.join(
60	    "third_party", "deep_research_bench", "data", "prompt_data", "query.jsonl"
61	)
62	
63	
64	def resolve_lineage(lineage: str | None) -> str:
65	    """Normalize + validate a lineage selector value (in-process default ``drb_ii_idx``).
66	
67	    ``None``/empty => the default ``drb_ii_idx`` (HEAD behavior). An unknown value FAILS LOUD
68	    (never a silent fallback to default — a typo'd selector must not silently score the wrong
69	    lineage). Callers pass this through the resolver so branching happens in ONE place.
70	    """
71	    value = (lineage or LINEAGE_DRB_II_IDX).strip() or LINEAGE_DRB_II_IDX
72	    if value not in ALLOWED_LINEAGES:
73	        raise GateZeroLineageError(
74	            f"GATE0: unknown {LINEAGE_SELECTOR_ENV} lineage {value!r} — allowed: "
75	            f"{sorted(ALLOWED_LINEAGES)}. Fix the selector (never guess the lineage)."
76	        )
77	    return value
78	
79	
80	def lineage_from_env() -> str:
81	    """The effective lineage from the env selector (in-process default ``drb_ii_idx``).
82	
83	    Read at CALL time (LAW VI) so a slate/operator override after import wins. The default is
84	    NOT written back to env, so a default run's serialized artifacts gain no selector key."""
85	    return resolve_lineage(os.getenv(LINEAGE_SELECTOR_ENV, "").strip() or None)
86	
87	# slug -> canonical DRB-II idx. The id<->idx offset is real (task72 -> idx 56, NOT 72);
88	# binding by idx is what prevents the drb_72 wrong-question failure. The slug strings here
89	# MUST be byte-identical to the launchable slugs in SWEEP_QUERIES (run_honest_sweep_r3.py) —
90	# bare "drb_76"/"drb_78" never matched the real "drb_76_gut_microbiota_crc"/"drb_78_parkinsons_dbs"
91	# slugs, so GATE0 silently skipped them (Codex iter-2 P1). Resolve any new slug from the gold
92	# file before adding it here; never guess.
93	SLUG_TO_IDX: dict[str, int] = {
94	    "drb_72_ai_labor": 56,
95	    "drb_75_metal_ions_cvd": 62,
96	    "drb_76_gut_microbiota_crc": 66,
97	    "drb_78_parkinsons_dbs": 72,
98	}
99	
100	# DRB-EN slugs the sweep launches that have NO DeepResearch-Bench-II gold task (no canonical
101	# idx, no info_recall rubric) — they CANNOT be canonically bound or coverage-scored. Listed
102	# EXPLICITLY so the fail-loud registration check does not trip on them and so the omission is
103	# documented, never a silent gap. drb_90 (ADAS liability) is simply not in the DRB-II gold file.
104	DRB_SLUGS_WITHOUT_CANONICAL_GOLD: frozenset[str] = frozenset({"drb_90_adas_liability"})
105	
106	# A DRB-EN benchmark slug looks like ``drb_<id>_<topic>``. Used to decide whether the
107	# registration check applies (non-benchmark sweep slugs are never gated).
108	_DRB_SLUG_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^drb_\d+")
109	
110	
111	def is_benchmark_slug(slug: str) -> bool:
112	    """True iff ``slug`` is a DRB-EN benchmark slug (``drb_<id>_...``)."""
113	    return bool(_DRB_SLUG_PATTERN.match(slug or ""))
114	
115	
116	def assert_drb_slug_registered(slug: str) -> None:
117	    """Fail loud if a ``drb_*`` slug is neither canonically mapped nor an explicit no-gold slug.
118	
119	    Catches a renamed/added benchmark slug (e.g. a future ``drb_NN``) that would otherwise
120	    launch a hardcoded question with NO canonical binding — the exact silent gap that let
121	    ``drb_76``/``drb_78`` (bare names) and ``drb_90`` slip past the GATE0 override. Non-benchmark
122	    slugs are ignored.
123	    """
124	    if not is_benchmark_slug(slug):
125	        return
126	    if slug in SLUG_TO_IDX or slug in DRB_SLUGS_WITHOUT_CANONICAL_GOLD:
127	        return
128	    raise GateZeroLineageError(
129	        f"GATE0: benchmark slug {slug!r} is UNREGISTERED — neither in SLUG_TO_IDX "
130	        f"(canonically bound) nor DRB_SLUGS_WITHOUT_CANONICAL_GOLD (explicit no-gold). "
131	        f"Resolve its gold idx from {DEFAULT_TASKS_PATH} and add it, or register it as no-gold. "
132	        f"Never let a benchmark slug launch a hardcoded question unbound (the drb_72 failure)."
133	    )
134	
135	
136	class GateZeroLineageError(RuntimeError):
137	    """Raised fail-loud when benchmark artifact lineage is violated.
138	
139	    A run that raises this is INVALID and must be excluded from any score. This is the
140	    structural guard against the drb_72 wrong-question / split-brain-scoring failures.
141	    """
142	
143	
144	def _normalize(text: str) -> str:
145	    """Whitespace-normalize a question for comparison (semantics preserved, layout ignored)."""
146	    return " ".join((text or "").split())
147	
148	
149	def sha256_text(text: str) -> str:
150	    """Stable sha256 of normalized text."""
151	    return hashlib.sha256(_normalize(text).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
152	
153	
154	def load_canonical_question(idx: int, tasks_path: str = DEFAULT_TASKS_PATH) -> str:
155	    """Return the canonical question prompt for a DRB-II idx, straight from the gold file.
156	
157	    Fails loud if the gold file is missing or the idx is not present — never returns a
158	    silent default.
159	    """
160	    if not os.path.isfile(tasks_path):
161	        raise GateZeroLineageError(
162	            f"GATE0: canonical tasks file not found: {tasks_path} — cannot verify lineage"
163	        )
164	    with open(tasks_path, encoding="utf-8") as handle:
165	        for line in handle:
166	            line = line.strip()
167	            if not line:
168	                continue
169	            record = json.loads(line)
170	            if record.get("idx") == idx:
171	                prompt = record.get("prompt")
172	                if not prompt:
173	                    raise GateZeroLineageError(
174	                        f"GATE0: idx={idx} present but has empty 'prompt' in {tasks_path}"
175	                    )
176	                return prompt
177	    raise GateZeroLineageError(
178	        f"GATE0: idx={idx} not found in canonical tasks file {tasks_path}"
179	    )
180	
181	
182	def load_legacy_task_question(
183	    task_id: int, legacy_tasks_path: str = DEFAULT_LEGACY_TASKS_PATH
184	) -> str:
185	    """Return the legacy DeepResearch-Bench (RACE) task prompt for a task ``id``, from query.jsonl.
186	
187	    The legacy file is keyed by ``id`` (NOT ``idx``) and carries the prompt under ``prompt`` —
188	    the SAME record score_report_race.py --task-id scores against. Fails loud if the file is
189	    missing or the id is absent (never a silent default), mirroring ``load_canonical_question``.
190	    """
191	    if not os.path.isfile(legacy_tasks_path):
192	        raise GateZeroLineageError(
193	            f"GATE0: legacy tasks file not found: {legacy_tasks_path} — cannot resolve the "
194	            f"legacy_race_task question (verify the third_party/deep_research_bench symlink)"
195	        )
196	    with open(legacy_tasks_path, encoding="utf-8") as handle:
197	        for line in handle:
198	            line = line.strip()
199	            if not line:
200	                continue
201	            record = json.loads(line)
202	            if record.get("id") == task_id:
203	                prompt = record.get("prompt")
204	                if not prompt:
205	                    raise GateZeroLineageError(
206	                        f"GATE0: legacy task id={task_id} present but has empty 'prompt' in "
207	                        f"{legacy_tasks_path}"
208	                    )
209	                return prompt
210	    raise GateZeroLineageError(
211	        f"GATE0: legacy task id={task_id} not found in legacy tasks file {legacy_tasks_path}"
212	    )
213	
214	
215	def canonical_question_for_slug(
216	    slug: str,
217	    tasks_path: str = DEFAULT_TASKS_PATH,
218	    *,
219	    lineage: str = LINEAGE_DRB_II_IDX,
220	    legacy_tasks_path: str = DEFAULT_LEGACY_TASKS_PATH,
221	) -> str:
222	    """Canonical question for a benchmark slug, per the ``lineage`` selector.
223	
224	    ``drb_ii_idx`` (default): the DeepResearch-Bench-II canonical question by idx (SLUG_TO_IDX +
225	    ``tasks_path``) — byte-identical to HEAD. ``legacy_race_task``: the legacy DeepResearch-Bench
226	    (RACE) task question (SLUG_TO_LEGACY_TASK id in ``legacy_tasks_path``). A legacy/slug pair with
227	    NO legacy mapping FAILS LOUD (never a guessed id). Positional ``(slug, tasks_path)`` stays valid;
228	    ``lineage``/``legacy_tasks_path`` are keyword-only.
229	    """
230	    resolved = resolve_lineage(lineage)
231	    if resolved == LINEAGE_LEGACY_RACE_TASK:
232	        if slug not in SLUG_TO_LEGACY_TASK:
233	            raise GateZeroLineageError(
234	                f"GATE0: slug {slug!r} has no legacy_race_task mapping — add it to "
235	                f"SLUG_TO_LEGACY_TASK (the legacy query.jsonl id) before running under "
236	                f"{LINEAGE_SELECTOR_ENV}={LINEAGE_LEGACY_RACE_TASK} (never guess the id)"
237	            )
238	        return load_legacy_task_question(SLUG_TO_LEGACY_TASK[slug], legacy_tasks_path)
239	    if slug not in SLUG_TO_IDX:
240	        raise GateZeroLineageError(
241	            f"GATE0: slug {slug!r} has no canonical idx mapping — resolve it from the gold "
242	            f"file and add it to SLUG_TO_IDX before running (never guess the idx)"
243	        )
244	    return load_canonical_question(SLUG_TO_IDX[slug], tasks_path)
245	
246	
247	def assert_launched_question_is_canonical(
248	    slug: str,
249	    launched_question: str,
250	    tasks_path: str = DEFAULT_TASKS_PATH,
251	    *,
252	    lineage: str = LINEAGE_DRB_II_IDX,
253	    legacy_tasks_path: str = DEFAULT_LEGACY_TASKS_PATH,
254	) -> str:
255	    """Fail loud unless the launched question equals the canonical question for the slug.
256	
257	    Returns the canonical question (so callers can launch THAT, not a hardcoded copy).
258	    This is the structural fix for the drb_72 wrong-question bug. ``lineage`` selects which
259	    canonical the launched question must equal (default ``drb_ii_idx`` = HEAD).
260	    """
261	    canonical = canonical_question_for_slug(
262	        slug, tasks_path, lineage=lineage, legacy_tasks_path=legacy_tasks_path
263	    )
264	    if sha256_text(launched_question) != sha256_text(canonical):
265	        raise GateZeroLineageError(
266	            "GATE0 WRONG-QUESTION: launched question != canonical for slug "
267	            f"{slug!r}.\n  launched(sha)={sha256_text(launched_question)[:16]} : "
268	            f"{_normalize(launched_question)[:120]!r}\n  canonical(sha)="
269	            f"{sha256_text(canonical)[:16]} : {_normalize(canonical)[:120]!r}"
270	        )
271	    return canonical
272	
273	
274	def assert_no_split_brain(
275	    slug: str,
276	    packed_question: str,
277	    answered_question: str,
278	    tasks_path: str = DEFAULT_TASKS_PATH,
279	    *,
280	    lineage: str = LINEAGE_DRB_II_IDX,
281	    legacy_tasks_path: str = DEFAULT_LEGACY_TASKS_PATH,
282	) -> None:
283	    """Fail loud unless packed == answered == canonical (the split-brain guard).
284	
285	    `packed_question` = the question handed to the judge/scorer pack.
286	    `answered_question` = the question the report (corpus_snapshot) actually answered.
287	    Both must equal the canonical question for the ``lineage`` (default ``drb_ii_idx`` = HEAD;
288	    ``legacy_race_task`` = the legacy query.jsonl task), or the score is meaningless.
289	    """
290	    canonical = canonical_question_for_slug(
291	        slug, tasks_path, lineage=lineage, legacy_tasks_path=legacy_tasks_path
292	    )
293	    c = sha256_text(canonical)
294	    p = sha256_text(packed_question)
295	    a = sha256_text(answered_question)
296	    if not (p == c and a == c):
297	        raise GateZeroLineageError(
298	            "GATE0 SPLIT-BRAIN: packed/answered/canonical question mismatch for slug "
299	            f"{slug!r}.\n  canonical(sha)={c[:16]}\n  packed(sha)   ={p[:16]} "
300	            f"({'OK' if p == c else 'MISMATCH'})\n  answered(sha) ={a[:16]} "
301	            f"({'OK' if a == c else 'MISMATCH'})"
302	        )
303	
304	
305	def build_lineage_manifest(
306	    *,
307	    slug: str,
308	    launched_question: str,
309	    packed_question: str,
310	    answered_question: str,
311	    rendered_report: str,
312	    judge_input: str,
313	    score_row: Any,
314	    backbone_model: str,
315	    decoding_params: dict[str, Any],
316	    prompt_template_id: str,
317	    retrieval_snapshot_id: str,
318	    judge_model_version: str,
319	    scorer_config_id: str,
320	    execution_seed: int,
321	    tasks_path: str = DEFAULT_TASKS_PATH,
322	    lineage: str = LINEAGE_DRB_II_IDX,
323	    legacy_tasks_path: str = DEFAULT_LEGACY_TASKS_PATH,
324	) -> dict[str, Any]:
325	    """Build the immutable lineage manifest for a benchmark run.
326	
327	    Hashes the full artifact chain (task -> question -> answer -> report -> judge input ->
328	    score) PLUS the reproducibility surface (backbone model, decoding, template, retrieval
329	    snapshot, judge model/version, scorer config, seed). Asserts canonical equality first (for
330	    the selected ``lineage``), so a manifest cannot be built for a wrong-question or split-brain
331	    run. Records the lineage; a ``legacy_race_task`` run is labelled by its legacy task id + is
332	    NEVER labelled ``canonical_idx`` (the DRB-II idx is a DIFFERENT lineage).
333	    """
334	    resolved_lineage = resolve_lineage(lineage)
335	    canonical = assert_launched_question_is_canonical(
336	        slug, launched_question, tasks_path,
337	        lineage=resolved_lineage, legacy_tasks_path=legacy_tasks_path,
338	    )
339	    assert_no_split_brain(
340	        slug, packed_question, answered_question, tasks_path,
341	        lineage=resolved_lineage, legacy_tasks_path=legacy_tasks_path,
342	    )
343	    if resolved_lineage == LINEAGE_LEGACY_RACE_TASK:
344	        # LEGACY: record the lineage + legacy task id; NEVER label it ``canonical_idx`` (that is
345	        # the DRB-II idx — a DIFFERENT lineage; labelling a legacy run canonical_idx=56 is the
346	        # split-brain this seam exists to prevent).
347	        lineage_label: dict[str, Any] = {
348	            "lineage": resolved_lineage,
349	            "legacy_task_id": SLUG_TO_LEGACY_TASK[slug],
350	        }
351	    else:
352	        # DEFAULT (drb_ii_idx): BYTE-IDENTICAL to HEAD — the manifest carries ``canonical_idx``
353	        # immediately after ``slug`` and gains NO ``lineage`` key (the default path adds no key).
354	        lineage_label = {
355	            "canonical_idx": SLUG_TO_IDX[slug],
356	        }
357	    return {
358	        "slug": slug,
359	        **lineage_label,
360	        "canonical_question_sha": sha256_text(canonical),
361	        "launched_question_sha": sha256_text(launched_question),
362	        "packed_question_sha": sha256_text(packed_question),
363	        "answered_question_sha": sha256_text(answered_question),
364	        "rendered_report_sha": sha256_text(rendered_report),
365	        "judge_input_sha": sha256_text(judge_input),
366	        "score_row_sha": sha256_text(json.dumps(score_row, sort_keys=True, default=str)),
367	        "backbone_model": backbone_model,
368	        "decoding_params_sha": sha256_text(json.dumps(decoding_params, sort_keys=True)),
369	        "prompt_template_id": prompt_template_id,
370	        "retrieval_snapshot_id": retrieval_snapshot_id,
371	        "judge_model_version": judge_model_version,
372	        "scorer_config_id": scorer_config_id,
373	        "execution_seed": execution_seed,
374	    }
